description: description
responsibilities: About the Expertise Centre – R&D Technologist The R&D Technologist Expertise Centre within ZS’s Drug Development practice partners with biopharma organizations to design and deliver innovative clinical data solutions that accelerate drug development and reduce risk from portfolio to patient. The team operates at the intersection of key ZS drug development verticals—including asset strategy, clinical trial design, clinical data management, statistical programming, regulatory, and risk-based quality. By combining deep clinical domain expertise, data standards, clinical programming, systems understanding, and consulting discipline, the team solves complex R&D challenges with speed, scale, and measurable impact. Director Expertise Center R&D Technologist – Clinical data solutions The Director – R&D Technologist (Clinical Data Solutions) provides strategic and scientific leadership across clinical data management, analytics, biostatistics, statistical programming, and clinical data standards. This role is central to the R&D Technologist Expertise Centre and focuses on designing and delivering innovative clinical data solutions across the drug development lifecycle—with a strong emphasis on clinical data standards, transformation, and analytics-driven decision-making. You will combine deep technical expertise, consulting leadership, and practice-building capabilities to serve as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, driving next-generation clinical data and analytics solutions that improve development outcomes and accelerate regulatory submissions What You’ll Do: Strategic & Client Leadership Provide strategic leadership across clinical data management, biometrics, and advanced analytics for end-to-end drug development programs Serve as ZS’s go-to leader for clinical data solutions, bringing a strong techno-functional perspective across clinical data flow and systems Lead complex client engagements spanning clinical data strategy, analytics enablement, and digital R&D transformation Facilitate senior-level workshops to align stakeholders on study design, data standards, analytics approaches, and operating models Clinical Data & Standards Leadership Bring deep expertise in clinical data standards (e.g., CDISC – CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF) Define innovative approaches to clinical data collection, transformation, and analytics Integrate clinical programming, analytics, and data science into trial design, execution, and regulatory workflows Drive adoption of standardized, scalable, and future-ready clinical data models Technology & Innovation Apply a techno-functional lens across clinical systems, data platforms, and enterprise architectures Leverage AI/ML and GenAI approaches to transform clinical data processing and analytics Define future-state operating models and digital ecosystems for clinical data and analytics Delivery Excellence & Governance Oversee quality, scientific rigor, and compliance of deliverables, including analytics, data models, and regulatory outputs Ensure alignment with GxP standards, regulatory expectations, and industry best practices Act as a bridge between scientific, business, and engineering teams Team & Practice Building Lead and grow a high-performing clinical data and analytics team Mentor senior talent and establish standards for delivery excellence Contribute to capability building, accelerators, and new offerings within the Expertise Centre Support business development through solutioning, client pursuits, and long-term relationship building
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Experience & Domain Expertise 18+ years of experience in Life Sciences R&D, with strong expertise in clinical data management, biometrics, and analytics Deep understanding of the end-to-end drug development lifecycle Strong domain knowledge of clinical data, analytics, and digital R&D transformation Clinical Systems & Platforms Experience working with: EDC Platforms: Medidata Rave, Veeva EDC, Oracle InForm Clinical Systems: ePRO, laboratory systems, CTMS, eTMF, CDMS Clinical Data Repositories: SAS LSAF, Oracle LSH, eClinical Elluminate Metadata Repositories: Nurocor, Sycamore, Formedix Statistical Computing: SAS, SAS Viya, Domino Data Standards & Interoperability Strong expertise in: CDISC: CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF Interoperability: FHIR, HL7, USDM Technical & Analytical Skills Working knowledge of R, Python, and SAS for clinical data transformation and analysis Exposure to AI/ML and GenAI-based solutions in clinical data and analytics Strong capability in business process modeling, data flow modeling, and solution architecture design Leadership & Consulting Skills Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with ability to influence senior leaders Proven experience in GxP-compliant, large-scale enterprise programs Ability to lead global, cross-functional, and multi-vendor teams Strong leadership, mentoring, and team-building capabilities Solid understanding of agile, product-led, and digital transformation delivery models

description: description
responsibilities: Architecture & Engineering Specialist – ML Engineering ZS’s India Capability & Expertise Center (CEC) houses more than 60% of ZS people across three offices in New Delhi, Pune and Bengaluru. Our teams work with colleagues across North America, Europe and East Asia to create and deliver real world solutions to the clients who drive our business. The CEC maintains standards of analytical, operational and technological excellence across our capability groups. Together, our collective knowledge enables each ZS team to deliver superior results to our clients. ZS's Scaled AI practice is part of ZS's rich and advanced AI ecosystem, in the Architecture & Engineering Expertise Center, focused on creating continuous business value for clients using a range of innovative machine learning, deep learning, and engineering capabilities.   Being part of Scaled AI practice allows you to collaborate with data scientists to create state-of-the-art AI models, create and use cutting-edge ML platforms, create and deploy advanced ML pipelines and manage the complete ML lifecycle. What You’ll Do Design and implement technical features leveraging best practices for technology stack being used Collaborate with client-facing teams to understand solution context and contribute to technical requirement gathering and analysis Work with technical architects on the team to validate design and implementation approach Write production-ready code that is easily testable, understood by other developers, and accounts for edge cases and errors Ensure the highest quality of deliverables by following architecture/design guidelines, coding best practices, periodic design/code reviews Write unit tests as well as higher-level tests to handle expected edge cases and errors gracefully, as well as happy paths Uses bug tracking, code review, version control, and other tools to organize and deliver work Participate in scrum calls and agile ceremonies, and effectively communicate work progress, issues, and dependencies Consistently contribute in researching & evaluating the latest technologies through rapid learning, conducting proofs-of-concept and creating prototype solutions Support the project architect in designing modules/component of the overall project/product architecture Breaks down large features into estimable tasks lead estimation and can defend them with clients Implement complex features with limited guidance from the engineering lead. For example service or application-wide change Systematically debug code issues/bugs using stack traces, logs, monitoring tools, and other resources Performs code/script reviews of senior engineers in the team Mentor and groom technical talent within the team What You’ll Bring At least 5+ relevant hands-on experience in deploying and productionizing ML models at scale  Expertise in Designing, configuring, and using ML Engineering platforms like Sagemaker, MLFlow, Kubeflow, or other platforms  Big data - Hive, Spark, Hadoop, queuing system like Apache Kafka/Rabbit MQ/AWS Kinesis Ability to quickly adapt to new technology and be innovative in creating solutions Ability to independently run POCs on new technologies and document findings to share Strong in at least one of the Programming languages - PySpark, Python or Java, Scala, etc. and Programming basics - Data Structures Hands-on experience in building metadata-driven, reusable design patterns for data pipeline, orchestration, ingestion patterns (batch, real-time) Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: What You’ll Do: Build, Refine and Use ML Engineering platforms and components. Scaling machine learning algorithms to work on massive data sets and strict SLAs. Build and orchestrate model pipelines including feature engineering, inferencing and continuous model training. Implement ML Ops including model KPI measurements, tracking, model drift & model feedback loop. Collaborate with client facing teams to understand business context at a high level and contribute in technical requirement gathering. Implement basic features aligning with technical requirements. Write production-ready code that is easily testable, understood by other developers and accounts for edge cases and errors. Ensure highest quality of deliverables by following architecture/design guidelines, coding best practices, periodic design/code reviews. Write unit tests as well as higher level tests to handle expected edge cases and errors gracefully, as well as happy paths. Uses bug tracking, code review, version control and other tools to organize and deliver work. Participate in scrum calls and agile ceremonies, and effectively communicate work progress, issues and dependencies. Consistently contribute in researching & evaluating latest architecture patterns/technologies through rapid learning, conducting proof-of-concepts and creating prototype solutions. What You’ll Bring A master's or bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field from a top university. 1-3 years’ hands-on experience in ML development. Good understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning Strong programming expertise in Python, PySpark/Scala. Expertise in crafting ML Models for high performance and scalability. Experience in implementing feature engineering, inferencing pipelines, and real time model predictions. Experience in ML Ops to measure and track model performance, experience working with MLFlow Experience with Spark or other distributed computing frameworks. Experience in ML platforms like Sage maker, Kubeflow. Experience with pipeline orchestration tools such Airflow. Experience in deploying models to cloud services like AWS, Azure, GCP, Azure ML. Expertise in SQL, SQL DB's. Knowledgeable of core CS concepts such as common data structures and algorithms. Collaborate well with teams with different backgrounds / expertise / functions Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collabrative spirit and probelm-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: Associate - Chemistry Manufacturing & Controls ZS’s Technology group focuses on scalable strategies, assets and accelerators that deliver to our clients enterprise-wide transformation via cutting-edge technology. We leverage digital and technology solutions to optimize business processes, enhance decision-making, and drive innovation. ZS is looking to hire a Business Technology Solutions Associate Consultant in ZS' Supply Chain and Manufacturing practice with experience in pharmaceutical drug development and tech transfer. The primary responsibility of this role is to deliver projects in the pharmaceutical CMC development area for ZS clients. The successful candidate will leverage their expertise in using and implementing digital laboratory systems such as Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), Electronic Lab Notebooks (ELN), and ensuring GLP-compliant solutions to support CMC workflows; along with supporting Digital and Technology advisory, Product and Platform development and Data, Analytics and AI implementation. What You’ll Do: Support clients in adopting and optimizing digital laboratory solutions (e.g., LIMS, ELN) and embedding GLP-aligned data management practices within CMC development workflows Configure, customize, and deploy LIMS platforms to support sample tracking, workflow automation, and regulatory reporting Implement and integrate ELN solutions for experiment documentation, protocol standardization, and collaboration across research and quality teams Ensure systems and processes comply with GLP standards, including audit readiness, data integrity, and alignment with regulatory requirements Partner with scientists, quality, and IT teams to digitize and scale laboratory workflows Undertake complete ownership in accomplishing activities and assigned responsibilities across all phases of project lifecycle to solve business problems across one or more client engagements. Apply appropriate development methodologies (e.g.: agile, waterfall) and best practices (e.g.: mid-development client reviews, embedded QA procedures, unit testing) to ensure successful and timely completion of assignments. Collaborate with other team members to leverage expertise and ensure seamless transitions; Exhibit flexibility in undertaking new and challenging problems and demonstrate excellent task management. Assist in creating project outputs such as business case development, solution vision and design, user requirements, prototypes, and technical architecture (if needed), test cases, and operations management. Bring transparency in driving assigned tasks to completion and report accurate status. Bring Consulting mindset in problem solving, innovation by leveraging technical and business knowledge/ expertise and collaborate across other teams. Assist senior team members, delivery leads in project management responsibilities. Build complex solutions using Programing languages, ETL service platform, etc. What You’ll Bring: Bachelor's or master's degree required in Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Material Engineering, Computer Science, Chemistry, Biotechnology or similar degree 1-3 years of relevant post-collegiate job experience. A PhD may substitute in lieu of work experience. Hands-on experience with at least one of the functions in CMC development such as Process Chemistry, Process Engineering, Formulation, and Analytical Chemistry Understanding of the different data and information systems such as ELN, LIMS, ERP systems, and LCM or PLM systems Experience contributing to the implementation of LIMS, ELN, or GLP-compliant digital systems, with focus on improving CMC data integrity, regulatory readiness, and lab-to-business process alignment Willingness to develop expertise in system configuration, validation, and compliance aspects of pharmaceutical digital lab solutions. Knowledge of key concepts including unit operations, processes, quality by design (QbD), tech transfer, process validation, quality attributes, and regulatory milestones across pharma development Understanding of the CMC related regulatory submission requirements Experience with at least one tech transfer, explore to analytics, AI and/or technology implementation in CMC preferred Strong ability to identify data anomalies, design data validation rules, and perform data cleanup to ensure high-quality data. Project management and task planning experience, ensuring smooth execution of deliverables and timelines. Strong communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with both technical and non-technical teams. Experience with automation testing High motivation, good work ethic, maturity and personal initiative. Self-discipline for planning and organizing tasks Aptitude for, and enjoyment of, working in teams Additional Skills: Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to structure thoughts logically during discussions and presentations Capability to simplify complex concepts into easily understandable frameworks and presentations Proficiency in working within a virtual global team environment, contributing to the timely delivery of multiple projects Travel to other offices as required to collaborate with clients and internal project team

description: description
responsibilities: Knowledge Services Group Overview Knowledge Services group offers a broad spectrum of Business Research, Pre-Sales and other knowledge-based services to clients and internal stakeholders. Knowledge Services has emerged as one of ZS’s distinctive competitive advantages, and a key enabler for our consultants to have lasting impact while addressing their clients' sales & marketing issues. Our services are based on deep research, and we bring together a unique combination of Business Research, Life-Sciences and Clinical experiences. What You’ll Do Assess firm knowledge needs and requirements (short-term or long-term) Deliver targeted insights using internal knowledge and external sources Help the firm prepare materials for Summits and Conferences Support the creation and update of knowledge assets including client updates, competitive intelligence, industry research etc. Maintain, manage, and develop the firm’s knowledge infrastructure Share best practices and tactical knowledge across offices, practice areas and team Engage and build relationships with firm members and leaders What You’ll Bring A track record in research and knowledge services, ready to support diverse ZS practice areas and project teams. 48+ months of relevant knowledge services experience in IT/Consulting companies; An academic background in life sciences, biotechnology with PhD only. Experience in working on databases like Evaluate Pharma, Adis, Avention, Datamonitor, Nexis etc; Proficiency in computer applications, including MS Office, with an interest in Enterprise application/BI tools; Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: What you'll do: Delivery Excellence Analyst in the Enterprise will… Support delivery teams in implementing Delivery Excellence practices, risk management frameworks, and quality standards across projects Assist in tracking and reporting operational KPIs; help monitor delivery performance metrics and flag risks/issues for timely escalation Support delivery risk governance activities by working with project teams and QRM stakeholders in tracking risks and mitigation actions Assist in organizing and conducting awareness sessions/trainings on Delivery Excellence practices and QMS process assets Support adherence to defined process maturity standards (e.g., CMMI Level 3) through documentation and tracking compliance Contribute to maintaining and updating Quality Management System (QMS) artifacts and templates Assist in driving consistency in delivery practices across projects through structured tracking and reporting Support adoption of new tools, processes, and systems by assisting teams and resolving basic queries Participate in project audits/reviews based on defined quality frameworks (e.g., CMMI), and assist in documenting observations, non-compliances, and best practices Fully aware of audit lifecycle for SDLC

A minimum of 2 years of experience in Delivery Assurance and Quality Management Knowledge and experience in Software Quality Management & Software Quality Standards Through understanding and implementation experience of CMMi 3.0 framework for Dev & Services Proficiency in MS Office, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. Ability to work independently and as part of a team Willingness to adapt to evolving industry standards and technologies Ability to manage a wide variety of tasks and meet deadlines, and reliability/dependability Proven ability to work creatively and analytically in a problem-solving environment Excellent time management skills. Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach

description: description
responsibilities: The Manager, CRM Business Transformation is a role within ZS's Customer Models & Motivation practice, responsible for supporting the business side of large-scale CRM transformation programs in life sciences. This role sits at the intersection of commercial strategy, customer engagement, and technology adoption—translating how biopharma organizations engage HCPs, patients, and field teams into clear business requirements, future-state processes, and organizational change plans that enable CRM platforms to deliver lasting value. Based in India, this role is primarily a delivery leadership position, responsible for managing and coordinating a team of analysts and consultants executing business transformation workstreams across global programs. The Manager will serve as the day-to-day delivery lead—ensuring quality, consistency, and progress across business process design, requirements management, and user journey work—while also engaging with client stakeholders in a supporting capacity alongside senior ZS leaders. This is not a technical implementation role. The Manager serves as the voice of the business and the field within the delivery team, ensuring that platform design reflects real commercial, medical, and field user needs. Close collaboration with ZS's onshore client leads, technology teams, and analytics teams is central to the role. What you'll do: Lead day-to-day delivery of business process design and future-state definition workstreams for commercial, medical affairs, and medical information/customer service functions as part of CRM platform implementations (e.g., Salesforce Life Sciences Cloud, Veeva). Manage, coach, and coordinate a team of analysts and consultants executing requirements gathering, process documentation, and user journey mapping across global and regional program workstreams. Oversee the end-to-end requirements management process—ensuring business requirements are clearly documented, stakeholder-reviewed, traceable, and kept current across the full scope of CRM capabilities being replaced or integrated. Direct the development of future-state user journeys by persona (Reps, MSLs, KAMs, Med Info, and others), translating how field and commercial roles will interact with the new platform into structured, prioritized business requirements. Facilitate discovery sessions, stakeholder workshops, and requirements interviews in coordination with onshore client-facing leads; prepare materials, synthesize outputs, and drive follow-through on open items and decisions. Ensure delivery quality across team workproducts including process documentation, user journey maps, requirements backlogs, change impact assessments, and stakeholder-facing presentations. Partner with organizational readiness and change management workstreams to ensure process changes, role impacts, and new ways of working are fully captured and reflected in training and adoption plans. Provide business enablement expertise to the broader program team, advising on role and organizational design implications of new commercial and medical processes, and helping navigate the balance between global harmonization and regional flexibility. Contribute to ZS business development by supporting proposal and RFP response development, and to the firm's thought leadership in life sciences CRM and commercial transformation.
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Bachelor's degree required; Master's degree highly preferred. 8+ years of experience in life sciences commercial, medical affairs, or operations roles—in industry, consulting, or a combination. Demonstrated experience leading delivery teams in a consulting or professional services environment; strong coaching, quality management, and workstream coordination skills. Deep familiarity with life sciences field team operations across sales, MSL, KAM, market access, and/or medical information functions; proven ability to capture and translate field user needs into structured business requirements. Experience on CRM implementation or transformation programs in life sciences; knowledge of Salesforce (Life Sciences Cloud or Health Cloud) and/or Veeva CRM strongly preferred. Proven ability to lead or support discovery workshops, stakeholder interviews, and requirements sessions with field users and commercial or medical leaders. Strong skills in business process documentation and user journey mapping; ability to produce clear, structured artifacts (process flows, requirements documents, user stories) that drive alignment across business and technical teams. Familiarity with organizational change management concepts and practices; ability to translate process and system changes into people and role impacts. Experience working on complex, multi-stakeholder global programs; comfort coordinating across global and regional teams in a distributed delivery model. Strong written and verbal communication skills; able to present clearly to senior clients and internal stakeholders in a supporting capacity. Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach

description: description
responsibilities: BTS Associate Consultant – MES /PIMS (Manufacturing) ZS is seeking an experienced MES / PIMS Subject Matter Expert who will drive the design, implementation, and optimization of Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) and Plant Information Management Systems (PIMS) within regulated pharmaceutical environments. This role focuses on bridging manufacturing operations, compliance, and technology, ensuring that digital solutions enable efficiency, traceability, and regulatory adherence. The ideal candidate combines hands‑on experience with MES/PIMS platforms and deep understanding of pharma manufacturing processes—from batch execution and electronic batch records to data contextualization and performance analytics. The position also demands familiarity with current Industry 4.0 integration frameworks, UNS, and MCP concepts for connected plants. What you’ll do: Lead or support the end to end design and deployment of MES and PIMS solutions aligning with manufacturing workflows (Batch Execution, EBR, Process Data Acquisition, OEE Monitoring). Serve as SME for pharma manufacturing operations, mapping process requirements into digital system configurations in compliance with GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, and Annex 11 guidelines. Collaborate with IT and automation teams to integrate MES/PIMS with related enterprise systems—ERP (SAP), LIMS, QMS, and data platforms—using standards such as ISA 95, OPC UA, and MQTT. Ensure system configurations meet validation and data integrity requirements (ALCOA+, CSV/CSA approach) and maintain appropriate documentation. Bridge plant floor equipment (PLC, SCADA, historians) data into enterprise applications through secure interfaces and data contextualization models. Contribute to initiatives involving Unified Namespace architectures, Manufacturing Connectivity Platforms, IIoT implementations, or cloud enabled MES solutions. Recommend enhancements to manufacturing data utilization, KPI definitions, and analytics enablement for process optimization and decision support. Partner with manufacturing, quality, automation, and IT stakeholders to harmonize processes and technology objectives across global operations. Engage with global manufacturing leaders on digital plant and manufacturing transformation programs, focusing on MES/PIMS modernization and IT OT convergence. Work with next generation Industry 4.0 frameworks, including Unified Namespace, MCP, digital twin, and advanced data analytics. Collaborate with experts in automation, data engineering, and architecture to deliver integrated and compliant manufacturing solutions. What you'll bring: 6–9 years of professional experience with at least 4 years in MES / PIMS functional deployment or manufacturing systems integration within pharmaceutical or life sciences manufacturing. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering (Computer Science, or Instrumentation) or related field. Preferred platform expertise: Werum PASX, Siemens Opcenter Execution (Process/Pharma), Rockwell FactoryTalk ProductionCentre, Emerson Syncade Strong understanding of ISA 95 manufacturing standards Knowledge of ETL processes, data models, and cloud based integration patterns (Azure, AWS) Awareness of UNS, MCP, and IIoT concepts supporting smart manufacturing initiatives Familiarity with cybersecurity and network segregation practices in regulated manufacturing environments Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach Additional Skills: Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to structure thoughts logically during discussions and presentations Capability to simplify complex concepts into easily understandable frameworks and presentations Proficiency in working within a virtual global team environment, contributing to the timely delivery of multiple projects Travel to other offices as required to collaborate with clients and internal project team

description: description
responsibilities: Project Management Associate Program Manager Expertise Center (PMEC) ZS's Program Management Expertise Center provides planning, delivery, and governance expertise to a wide range of clients and internal stakeholders. Our global organization is comprised of team-oriented, pragmatic, and results-driven people who thrive on solving challenging problems and driving the implementation of critical solutions. Our people come from diverse backgrounds, but share a passion for ensuring successful delivery of planned outcomes for our customers - both external clients as well as internal practice areas and industry vertical. What You’ll Do: Update project performance summaries (e.g., financials, utilization, SLA metrics) regularly; Own PMO processes and tools (e. g, project charter, ticket management system, issue management, communication and reporting, knowledge management, etc.) Prepare & Maintain Weekly Adhoc Tracking Reports; Facilitate continuous improvements leveraging various internal assets in consensus with key stakeholders; Maintain project documents repository and ensure timely updates and version control; Coordinate with team members to track the adherence on project processes (e.g., project status on ticket management system, etc.); Follow-up with client’s PMO team on any issues with the PMO tools/processes that are shared across ZS and client (e.g., ticket management system, etc.) and work with them on updating and sharing performance summaries with key stakeholders. What You’ll Bring: Graduate/post-graduate in any discipline with 1 to 3 years of experience in Project Management experience; Prior experience with program and PMO activity for large business transformations, or implementation of Digital strategy; experience managing large PMO vertical and client accounts; Any Life Science, Healthcare, Pharma or Consulting experience is a nice to have; Insurance or Finance industry experience also a plus; Strong understanding of project management phases, techniques, and tools & reporting; Ability to work in Agile, Waterfall and hybrid methodologies; Strong knowledge of PMI and Agile project management standards and terminologies. Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach. Additional Skills: PMI/PMP, Prince2, or Agile certification desired; Ability to understand projects KPIs and SLAs; Experience managing multiple projects at once and ensuring priority of issues and risks

description: description
responsibilities: Business Technology Solutions Associate Consultant Location: Pune/Gurgaon Region:INDIA-TECH-BT-2 Category: Technology Subcategory: R&D What you’ll do: Develop and deploy end-to-end AI solutions across various domains. • Design and implement deep learning models including Transformers. • Utilize large language models for diverse applications. • Work with databases and vector databases to manage and analyze data. • Leverage frameworks and packages for AI solution development. • Implement AI solutions on cloud platforms like Azure and AWS. • Develop containerized applications using Docker and other containerization tools. • Automate and streamline processes using various tools and technologies. • Contribute to projects involving AI-driven solutions for enhanced efficiency and performance
qualifications: What you’ll bring: • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field. • 3+ years of experience in AI and machine learning, specifically in computer vision, generative AI, and NLP. • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and C. • Strong experience with deep learning and large language models. • Familiarity with various databases and vector databases. • Hands-on experience with cloud services and containerization. • Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work in a team environment. • Strong communication skills to interact with stakeholders and team members effectively Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach Additional Skills: Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to structure thoughts logically during discussions and presentations Capability to simplify complex concepts into easily understandable frameworks and presentations Proficiency in working within a virtual global team environment, contributing to the timely delivery of multiple projects Travel to other offices as required to collaborate with clients and internal project teams

role: Wholesale Credit Risk Product Owner – Vice President
description:
As part of Risk Management and Compliance, you are at the center of keeping JPMorgan Chase strong and resilient. You help the firm grow its business in a responsible way by anticipating new and emerging risks and using your expert judgement to solve real-world challenges that impact our company, customers, and communities. Our culture in Risk Management and Compliance is all about thinking outside the box, challenging the status quo, and striving to be best-in-class.

In this role, you will be a key member of the Product Team within Wholesale Credit Risk. You will collaborate with technology partners as well other Product Teams in the program that are developing the underlying components that power the platform, to ensure they meet users' needs and those that offer the greatest value are prioritized.

role: Sr Lead Software Engineer - Python,  Spark, Backend development,AWS
description:
We have an opportunity to impact your career and provide an adventure where you can push the limits of what's possible.

As a Sr Lead Software Engineer at JPMorganChase within the corporate Technology’s Data Platform Engineering organization, you are an integral part of an agile team that works to enhance, build, and deliver trusted market-leading technology products in a secure, stable, and scalable way. As a core technical contributor, you are responsible for conducting critical technology solutions across multiple technical areas within various business functions in support of the firm’s business objectives.
